summary: Large ongoing baselib matching pass. Most of the diff reinterpreted raw sysdolphin/baselib assembly data from opaque .4byte hex into semantic assembler directives such as .ascii, .int, .float, and NULL, backed by a new tools/convert_data.py helper. The PR also added small Dolphin OS reset/reset-switch headers and C stubs, expanded baselib_unknown_002.c with OSResetSW-related structs, callback typedefs, externs, and prototypes, and adjusted PAD/MSL declarations needed by the matching work. The PR body explicitly said it could be merged at any time; there were no review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Converted large regions of baselib assembly data from raw .4byte hexadecimal to more meaningful directives, e.g. the bytes for "HAL Laboratory, Inc." became an .ascii string followed by NULL, 0x40000000 became .float 2.0, and small/negative values became .int forms.

Added tools/convert_data.py, a one-off Python data conversion tool that scans .4byte/NULL runs and rewrites them as ASCII strings, NULLs, floats, signed ints, unsigned ints, or fallback hex using configurable heuristics.
Added include/dolphin/os/OSReset.h with OSResetSystem, and moved __start.h to include that header instead of carrying a local extern declaration.
Added include/dolphin/os/OSResetSW.h with OSResetSW_Unk1, OSResetSW_Predicate, OSResetSW_MulticastPredicate, and OSGetResetSwitchState declarations.
Added minimal src/dolphin/os/OSReset.c and OSResetSW.c stubs tied to the new headers and known addresses/comments.
Expanded include/dolphin/pad/pad.h with PADClamp, PADRead, and PADReset declarations and cleaned spacing in PADStatus/SISetSamplingRate.

Expanded src/sysdolphin/baselib/baselib_unknown_002.c with additional includes, callback typedefs, reset-switch-related externs, arrays, and prototypes around functions such as func_80397520, func_80396C78, func_80396A20, func_80395550, func_80394E8C, func_80393D2C, and func_80394434.
Removed a stale commented function entry for func_80397520 from include/functions.h after declaring it in the baselib source context.
Added a decomp.me scratch link comment for OSGetResetSwitchState in asm/dolphin/os/OSResetSW.s.
smart_moves: Used semantic assembler directives to make raw data blocks readable without changing their binary meaning; this helps future decompilers recognize strings, floats, signed constants, sentinels, and tables instead of treating everything as anonymous hex.

Added a conversion script with configurable heuristics rather than manually editing thousands of .4byte lines, reducing clerical errors and making the transformation repeatable for similar data sections.
Introduced narrow headers for OSReset and OSResetSW so prototypes could be shared cleanly instead of duplicated in unrelated headers such as __start.h.

Kept uncertain structures honest by naming them OSResetSW_Unk1 and using offset-style fields such as x0_maybe_color, xC_buffer, xD4, and pad arrays; this preserves layout evidence while avoiding overclaiming semantics.

Moved matching context into src/sysdolphin/baselib/baselib_unknown_002.c through typed callback typedefs and extern declarations, which is a practical bridge between assembly-only regions and later C decompilation.
decomp_lessons: When a data section is still in assembly, converting .4byte runs into .ascii/.asciz, .float, .int, and NULL can reveal table structure and improve future C reconstruction while retaining exact emitted bytes.

A purpose-built data conversion script should expose thresholds such as minimum ASCII length, signed/unsigned integer ranges, and float magnitude/digit limits because false positives are possible in mixed binary data.
For unknown structs, offset-preserving names and explicit padding ranges are preferable to guessed semantic names; they allow matching work to proceed while documenting what offsets are known.
Create real subsystem headers as soon as external functions are used in multiple places; this PR moved OSResetSystem out of a local __start.h extern and into include/dolphin/os/OSReset.h.
Callback-heavy baselib code benefits from typedefs for function pointer shapes even before names are known, because typedefs make extern arrays and struct fields easier to compare against assembly.
Document decomp.me scratch links near the relevant assembly symbol when they are part of the matching investigation.
naming_conventions: Unknown baselib structs and callbacks used numbered names such as baselib_Unk12, baselib_Unk14, baselib_UnkCallback1, baselib_UnkCallbackContainer1, baselib_Unk17, and baselib_Unk18.
Unknown OS reset switch types used subsystem-qualified names such as OSResetSW_Unk1, OSResetSW_Predicate, and OSResetSW_MulticastPredicate.
Struct fields preserved offset information with x-prefixed names and padding ranges, e.g. x0_maybe_color, xC_buffer, x54_buffer, xBC, xC4, xD0, xD4, and x18_pad[0x54 - 0x18].
Extern labels retained lbl_ address names and address comments, e.g. lbl_804CF810, lbl_8040BD74, lbl_8040BC3C, and comments such as /* 004CC3F0 */.
Header guards followed project/subsystem style such as DOLPHIN_OS_OSRESET_H, DOLPHIN_OS_OSRESETSW_H, and SYSDOLPHIN_BASELIB_SYNTH_H.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced raw string bytes with .ascii "HAL Laboratory, Inc." plus NULL.
Replaced zero dwords with .4byte NULL where treated as pointer/null sentinel data.
Replaced float bit patterns with readable floats such as .float 2.0, .float 784.0, .float 776.0, .float -1.9375, .float 176.0, and .float -128.0.
Replaced signed two's-complement hex with .int negative values such as .int -5, .int -1, .int -704, .int -752, and .int -128.
Replaced small positive hex constants with .int values such as .int 381, .int 255, .int 767, .int 580, and .int 648.
Added tools/convert_data.py functions try_get_ascii, try_get_zero, try_get_f32, try_get_s32, try_get_u32, and get_dword_hex to classify each buffered dword run.
Added a scratch reference comment '# https://decomp.me/scratch/W02Di' immediately before OSGetResetSwitchState.
Used typed prototypes for reset-switch predicates and callback containers to support C-side matching of baselib_unknown_002.c.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only PR body guidance was: "Can be merged at any time."
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace OSResetSW_Unk1 or baselib_Unk* with named structs.
Find later PRs that remove or update tools/convert_data.py after data conversion work.
Search for OSGetResetSwitchState and scratch W02Di to see whether the function was later matched in C.
Search for lbl_804CF810, lbl_8040BD74, and lbl_8040BC3C to track reset-switch table naming.
Check later baselib_unknown_001.s and baselib_unknown_002.s diffs for further .4byte-to-typed-data conversions.
